question.text: Which of the following statements about a Certificate of Deposit (CD) is correct?

answer.text: CDs can be issued by Regional Rural Banks (RRBs)
The minimum denomination for a CD is Rs. 5 lakh
CDs issued by banks have a maturity range of 7 days to 1 year
NRIs can subscribe to CDs on a repatriable basis

explanation.text: **The Logic:** CDs issued by scheduled commercial banks (excluding RRBs and LABs) have a maturity range of 7 days to 1 year. CDs issued by select financial institutions have a maturity of 1 to 3 years. Minimum denomination is Rs. 1 lakh, and NRIs can subscribe on a non-repatriable basis only. **The Trap:** Option A is wrong because RRBs and Local Area Banks are explicitly excluded from issuing CDs. Option D is a common trap — NRIs can invest but only on non-repatriable basis, not repatriable. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IV (Interest Rate Derivatives) and Series V-B (MF Foundation) test CD characteristics in detail. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Remember the key CD facts: banks = 7 days to 1 year, FIs = 1-3 years, minimum Rs. 1 lakh, NRIs non-repatriable. These four facts cover most exam questions on CDs.
